title: 401(k)/403(b) Contribution Limits
content:
401(k) & 403(b) Contribution Limits for 2025

Amount you can put in via payroll $23,500
Additional if you’re age 50 or older $7,500
Additional in the year you turn 60, 61, 62, or 63.  $11,250
Total limit between you and your employer contributions $70,000 ($77,500 for 50 - 59 or 64+, and $81,250 for those turning 60, 61, 62, or 63)

title: HSA and FSA contribution limits
content:
Health Savings Account (HSA) Contribution Limits

2025 2026
Limit for individual coverage $4,300 $4,400
Limit for family coverage $8,550 $8,750
Additional if you’re 55 or older* $1,000 $1,000

*If both spouses are over 55, need a second account for an additional $1,000 for the second spouse

Healthcare Flexible Spending Account (FSA) Contribution Limits

2025 2026
$3,300 $3,400

title: IRA Income & Contribution limits
content:
For the 2025 Tax Year

Issue Roth IRA Deductible Traditional IRA*
Income Limitations

Full Contribution:

  • For single filers, if MAGI is less than $150,000
  • For couples Married Filing Jointly if MAGI is less than $236,000

Partial Contribution:

  • For single filers, if MAGI is between $150,000 and $165,000
  • For couples Married Filing Jointly if MAGI is between $236,000 and $246,000

Fully Deductible:

  • For single filers, if MAGI is less than $79,000
  • For couples Married Filing Jointly if MAGI is less than $126,000

Partially Deductible:

  • For single filers, if MAGI is between $79,000 and $89,000
  • For couples Married Filing Jointly if MAGI is between $126,000 and $146,000
Contribution Limit Lesser of 100 percent of earned income or $7,000 (must aggregate between traditional IRA and Roth IRA).
Catch-up Provision Additional $1,000 contribution if age 50 or older.

For the 2026 Tax Year

Issue Roth IRA Deductible Traditional IRA*
Income Limitations 

Full Contribution:

  • For single filers, if MAGI is less than $153,000
  • For couples Married Filing Jointly if MAGI is less than $242,000

Partial Contribution:

  • For single filers, if MAGI is between $153,000 and $168,000
  • For couples Married Filing Jointly if MAGI is between $242,000 and $252,000

Fully Deductible:

  • For single filers, if MAGI is less than $81,000
  • For couples Married Filing Jointly if MAGI is less than $129,000

Partially Deductible:

  • For single filers, if MAGI is between $81,000 and $91,000
  • For couples Married Filing Jointly if MAGI is between $129,000 and $149,000
Contribution Limit Lesser of 100 percent of earned income or $7,500 (must aggregate between traditional IRA and Roth IRA).
Catch-up Provision Additional $1,100 contribution if age 50 or older.

*No income limitations if NOT covered by an employer-sponsored retirement plan
notes: Contains prior and current year income and contribution limits

title: How Assets Pass To Heirs
Social Security… When to Take It
content:
Age to collect full benefits

65 65+ 66 66+ 67
Born before 12/31/1937 Born between 1938-1942 Born between 1943-1954 Born between 1955-1959 Born in ​
1960 or after

The 2025 maximum benefit @ FRA is $4,018 per month.​

The average estimated benefit is $1,976 per month.

title: The Bucket Strategy
Retirement Timeline
HSA contribution limits
content:
Health Savings Account (HSA) Contribution Limits*

2025 2026
Limit for individual coverage $4,300 $4,400
Limit for family coverage $8,550 $8,750
Additional catch-up contribution if age 55 or older** $1,000 $1,000

*Includes both employer and employee contributions.

**If married, each spouse is eligible for catch-up contributions starting in the year they turn 55. Each spouse must make catch-up contributions to their own separate HSA.
notes: This snippet includes the current and prior year limits for individual and family coverage, along with the catch-up amount for those age 55+.

title: 457 Contribution Limits
content:
457 Contribution Limits for 2025

Amount you can put in via payroll

Lesser of $23,500 or 100% of participant’s includible compensation

Catch-up contribution

$7,500

Age 60-63 catch-up contribution 

$11,250

Total limit between you and your employer contributions

$23,500 ($31,000 for 50+; $34,750 for 60-63) or less*

*Limit is capped at 100% of annual salary

Special catch-up provision for workers 50 or older in the three years before reaching retirement age (as defined in the plan)

May contribute the lesser of twice the annual limit ($47,000) or the annual limit plus unused eligible contribution limits from prior years
